authentication.name: Authentication & Authorization
explanations: Authentication is the first line of defense against unauthorized access
Weak passwords can be easily cracked using brute force or dictionary attacks
Default credentials are often left unchanged and are a common vulnerability
Proper session management prevents session hijacking and fixation attacks

authorization.name: Access Control
explanations: Authorization ensures users can only access resources they're permitted to
Broken access control can lead to unauthorized data access or modification
Privilege escalation allows low-privilege users to gain admin access

session_management.name: Session Management
explanations: Session management is crucial for maintaining user authentication state
Weak session tokens can be predicted or stolen by attackers
Proper session timeout prevents session hijacking attacks

input_validation.name: Input Validation
explanations: Input validation prevents malicious data from being processed by the application
SQL injection can lead to database compromise and data theft
XSS attacks can steal user cookies and session information
Command injection can allow attackers to execute system commands

file_upload.name: File Upload Security
explanations: File upload vulnerabilities can allow attackers to upload malicious files
Proper file type validation prevents execution of dangerous file types
Path traversal can allow access to sensitive files outside the upload directory

api_security.name: API Security
explanations: APIs need proper authentication to prevent unauthorized access
Rate limiting prevents API abuse and denial of service attacks
Proper input validation prevents injection attacks through APIs

error_handling.name: Error Handling
explanations: Proper error handling prevents information disclosure to attackers
Custom error pages improve user experience and security
Error logging helps in detecting and investigating security incidents

logging.name: Security Logging
explanations: Security logging is essential for detecting and investigating security incidents
Logs should be protected against tampering and unauthorized access
Proper log monitoring helps in early detection of security breaches

risk_levels.critical: Immediate action required - System compromise likely
risk_levels.high: Urgent attention needed - Significant security risk
risk_levels.medium: Should be addressed - Moderate security risk
risk_levels.low: Can be scheduled - Minor security issue

categories.injection: SQL injection, Command injection, LDAP injection
categories.authentication: Login issues, Session management, Access control
categories.sensitive_data: Data exposure, Encryption issues, Privacy violations
